Optical Media Notes

 

There are several methods of getting files onto your debug kit for the Xbox® video game system from Microsoft® or onto the Xbox Development Kit (XDK) for the purpose of validating latest game bits:

•         Placing files on the hard disk using tools included with the XDK, such as XBCP and the Microsoft® Windows® Explorer Shell Extension for Xbox.

•         Creating an Xbox game disc image using the GDFUtil tool provided in the XDK. This image can then be burned onto single layer optical media (CD-RW or DVD-R/DVD-RW) for the purpose of testing.

•         Simulating the performance characteristics of an actual dual layer Xbox game disc using the emulation feature of the Xbox Game Disc Layout Tool with an XDK.

Optical Media

The GDFUTIL tool supplied with the XDK can be used to produce a GDF image of the necessary game files. Once a GDF image has been prepared, it should be possible to use standard burning utilities (usually bundled with optical burners) to place the GDF image on CD-RW, DVD-R, or DVD-RW.

Supported CD-RW Media

Brand Name

Recording speed

TDK CD-RW 650 MB 70 Min

1-4X

TDK CD-RW 700 MB 80 Min

1-4X

BASF CD-RW 650 MB 70 Min

1-4X

IMPEGA CD-RW 65 0MB 70 Min

1-4X

Philips CD-RW 650 MB 70 Min

1-4X

CD-RW burners should be used at a burn rate compatible with the speeds noted in the preceding table. CD-RW media that is burned at rates faster than 4X will not be readable in an Xbox.

Supported DVD-R / DVD-RW Media

There are two primary types of DVD-R media currently available on the market that will work with the Xbox. These types are referred to as DVD-R for Authoring and DVD-R for General. General media is the recommended DVD-R media type for use with the Xbox given its future potential and lower cost.

The primary differences between the two types of DVD-R media are the recording wavelength, cutting master format (CMF) support, and cost. Authoring media uses a wavelength of 635 nm for recording, supports CMF, and is the more expensive of the two types of DVD-R media. General media uses a wavelength of 650 nm for recording (650 nm lasers are already commonly used in DVD players so cost of laser is significantly less), doesn't support CMF, and is the more cost effective of the two types of DVD-R media. CMF support is not an issue when deciding what type of media to use because CMF, which is used only during the mastering process, is not supported by the mastering process for Xbox game discs.

There are currently two DVD-R burners available on the market: the Pioneer DVR-S201 burner, the Pioneer DVR-A03 (DVR-103 OEM) burner, and the Pioneer DVR-A05 (DVR-105 OEM) burner. The Pioneer DVR-S201 can be used to produce DVD-R media for Xbox if it has not been modified by a third party for the production of platform-specific DVD-R media. However, the Pioneer DVR-A03 (DVR-103) and DVR-A05 (DVR-105) are recommended for creating DVD-R media to be used in the Xbox. They are capable of burning General DVD-R, General DVD-RW, CD-R, and CD-RW.

Authoring 4.7-GB Media

The following Authoring media have been tested with the Xbox:

Capacity

4.7 GB

DLT Media

Game submissions for pre-certification and final certification are required to be made on DLTtape® IV media. The layout of a game is created with the Xbox Game Disc Layout Tool included in the XDK. Game layout can be tested through the use of the emulation feature included in the Xbox Game Disc Layout Tool. Once the layout of the game has been completed and tested, the DLTtape IV media is created using the Xbox Premastering Wizard. This wizard is another feature available from the Xbox Game Disc Layout Tool.

The Xbox Premastering Wizard will create a single DLTtape IV for each Xbox game disc associated with a game. The user will no longer be required to create two pieces of DLTtape IV media for each Xbox game disc as earlier communicated. Instead, both layers of the Xbox game disc will be consolidated onto a single tape. The tapes used for submission must be of DLTtape IV format recorded in 35-GB uncompressed or 40-GB uncompressed format. Neither compressed tapes nor DLTtape III format tapes will be accepted.

Acceptable media for certification submissions:

Brand Name

Type

Format

Compression

Any brand displaying the official DLTtape logo from Quantum Corporation

DLTtape IV

35.0/40.0 GB

Uncompressed

DLT 8000 Drives

The PC running the Xbox Premastering Wizard must be connected to a DLT 8000 drive. The DLT 8000 tape drive will be a SCSI device, so the PC will also need a suitable SCSI card (for example, Adaptec 29160) installed so that the DLT 8000 tape drive can be connected. It should be noted that the SCSI-2-like connector on the Xbox DVD Emulator PCI card is NOT a true SCSI connector and should not be used to connect the DLT 8000 tape drive. A separate SCSI card will be required. Tapes created with lower-end DLT drives, such as DLT 4000 drives, will not be accepted for game submissions.

Brand Name

Type

Any with official DLTtape logo from Quantum Corporation

DLT 8000
